# Tools reference

<!-- Generated by scripts/export-tools-json.py. Do not edit by hand. -->

This page is generated from the MCP app's live tool registry. Regenerate it when tool names, descriptions, input schemas, or availability rules change.

Generated tool count: **62**.

The machine-readable schema is committed at [`generated/tools.json`](generated/tools.json).

Tool availability still depends on client capability and server settings. Regular connector-style clients may only expose `search` and `fetch`; ChatGPT Developer Mode and full MCP clients can expose the complete MCP surface. Remote tools require remote workers to be enabled and connected. Agent bridge tools require agent bridge configuration.

## Read-only connector tools

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `fetch` | `id` (string, required) | Fetch one workspace UTF-8 text file as a connector-compatible document. |
| `search` | `query` (string, required) | Search workspace text files and return connector-compatible result cards. Use this for connector-style document retrieval clients that expect a search -> fetch workflow. For code navigation or precise workspace inspection, use tools such as grep_search, glob_search, tree_view, read_file, or read_many_files. Parameter: query is a case-insensitive literal text query. The tool searches from the workspace root, returns at most one result card per matched file, and each card id is the value to pass to fetch. |

## Environment and safety

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `environment_info` | none | Return the active workspace, server settings, auth/policy state, and a small environment probe. Use this before planning tool-heavy work when you need to know the workspace root, auth/network policy, or basic runtime versions. This is read-only and intended for orientation, not for executing arbitrary commands. |
| `secret_scan` | `cwd` (string, optional)<br>`glob` (string | null, optional)<br>`max_results` (integer, optional) | Scan workspace text files for common secret-like strings before commit, push, release, or sharing logs. Results are heuristic and do not prove the workspace is secret-free. Current max findings: 200. |

## Shell and Python

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `kill_persistent_shell` | `session_id` (string, required) | Terminate a persistent shell session by session_id. Use when a server, watch process, REPL, or stuck command is no longer needed. This is destructive for that session but does not delete files. |
| `list_persistent_shells` | none | List active persistent shell sessions. Use before reading, sending to, or killing sessions when you do not know the session_id or need to check what long-running processes are active. |
| `read_persistent_shell_output` | `lines` (integer, optional)<br>`session_id` (string, required) | Read recent output from a persistent shell session. Use after start_persistent_shell or send_persistent_shell_input to inspect incremental output without blocking. Parameters: session_id must be an id returned by start_persistent_shell or list_persistent_shells; lines defaults to 200 and controls how many recent lines are returned. Increase lines only when needed for context. |
| `run_python_code` | `code` (string, required)<br>`cwd` (string, optional)<br>`timeout_s` (integer, optional) | Write Python code to a temporary file and execute it in the controlled workspace/container. Use for short scripts, structured file analysis, JSON manipulation, or calculations that are easier and safer in Python than shell. Current timeout cap: 60 seconds. Current combined output cap: 200000 bytes. Keep code non-interactive and write durable outputs explicitly if needed. |
| `run_shell_command` | `command` (string, required)<br>`cwd` (string, optional)<br>`max_output_bytes` (integer | null, optional)<br>`timeout_s` (integer | null, optional) | Run one bounded, non-interactive shell command in the controlled workspace/container. Use for build, test, package-manager, git, and inspection commands that should finish promptly. Current public timeout default/cap: 10/60 seconds. Current combined output cap: 200000 bytes. For long-running, interactive, or streaming processes, use the persistent shell tools. |
| `send_persistent_shell_input` | `enter` (boolean, optional)<br>`input_text` (string, required)<br>`session_id` (string, required) | Send input to an existing persistent shell session. Use after start_persistent_shell when a process is waiting for commands or interactive input. Set enter=false only when intentionally sending partial input without a newline. |
| `start_persistent_shell` | `command` (string | null, optional)<br>`cwd` (string, optional)<br>`name` (string | null, optional) | Start a persistent tmux-backed shell session. Use for interactive programs, development servers, REPLs, long-running watches, or commands whose output must be read incrementally. Parameters: cwd defaults to '.' and is resolved relative to the workspace unless an allowed absolute path is supplied; name is an optional human-readable session label; command is optional and starts immediately in the session. For one-shot commands, use run_shell_command. |

## Filesystem and search

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `apply_patch` | `cwd` (string, optional)<br>`patch` (string, required) | Apply a unified diff using git apply. |
| `delete_file_or_dir` | `path` (string, required)<br>`recursive` (boolean, optional) | Delete a file or directory inside the controlled workspace/container. |
| `edit_file` | `new` (string, required)<br>`old` (string, required)<br>`path` (string, required)<br>`replace_all` (boolean, optional) | Replace exact text in a validated UTF-8 text file. Use for small precise edits after reading the target file. Current write cap: 5000000 bytes. For larger or multi-file changes, prefer apply_patch. |
| `glob_search` | `cwd` (string, optional)<br>`max_results` (integer, optional)<br>`pattern` (string, required) | Find files by glob pattern when you know filename patterns and need matching paths, not file contents. Current max glob results: 5000. |
| `grep_search` | `case_sensitive` (boolean, optional)<br>`cwd` (string, optional)<br>`glob` (string | null, optional)<br>`max_results` (integer | null, optional)<br>`query` (string, required)<br>`regex` (boolean, optional) | Search file contents with ripgrep to locate symbols, usages, error messages, or text before reading or editing files. Current max_grep_results=200. |
| `list_files` | `max_entries` (integer, optional)<br>`path` (string, optional)<br>`recursive` (boolean, optional) | List files and directories under a path for quick inspection. The result reports whether entries were truncated by the requested limit or server cap. Current max directory entries: 5000. |
| `multi_edit_file` | `edits` (array, required)<br>`path` (string, required) | Apply multiple exact-text edits to one file. |
| `read_file` | `end_line` (integer | null, optional)<br>`path` (string, required)<br>`start_line` (integer | null, optional) | Read a UTF-8 text file, optionally by line range. Use after locating a file to inspect exact content before editing. Current per-file read cap: 512000 bytes. |
| `read_many_files` | `files` (array, required) | Read multiple UTF-8 text files with optional per-file line ranges. Use for targeted context gathering across known paths. Current limits: 100 files and 5000000 returned bytes. |
| `tree_view` | `cwd` (string, optional)<br>`depth` (integer, optional)<br>`max_entries` (integer, optional) | Return a compact directory tree for high-level project orientation before targeted file reads. Current max tree entries: 5000. |
| `write_file` | `content` (string, required)<br>`overwrite` (boolean, optional)<br>`path` (string, required) | Write a full UTF-8 text file. Use to create a new file or intentionally replace a whole file. Current write cap: 5000000 bytes. For precise modifications, prefer edit_file or apply_patch. |

## File download links

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `create_file_link` | `filename` (string | null, optional)<br>`max_downloads` (integer | null, optional)<br>`path` (string, required)<br>`ttl_s` (integer | null, optional) | Create a temporary tokenized HTTP download URL for one existing regular file in the controlled workspace/container. Use this when a user needs to download or open an artifact through a browser. The response includes a sensitive token and URL. Current TTL default/cap: 3600/604800 seconds. Current file-size cap: 0 bytes, with 0 meaning no configured cap. |
| `list_file_links` | `include_expired` (boolean, optional) | List tokenized file download links created by create_file_link. |
| `revoke_file_link` | `token` (string, required) | Revoke a tokenized file download link created by create_file_link. |

## Todo state

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `read_todos` | none | Read the current agent todo list. |
| `write_todos` | `todos` (array, required) | Replace the current structured agent todo list. |

## Remote worker management

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `remote_environment_info` | `machine` (string, required) | Return workspace, auth, policy, and basic environment information from a remote worker. Use to verify the remote machine, working directory, runtime versions, and limits before running remote commands or editing remote files. |
| `remote_invite` | `name` (string | null, optional)<br>`ttl_s` (integer | null, optional)<br>`workdir` (string | null, optional) | Create a one-time command for a remote machine to join this control server. Use when you need to run workspace tools on another worker. Defaults: ttl_s defaults to the configured remote_invite_ttl_s=600 seconds when omitted. Security: treat the invite as sensitive because it grants enrollment capability. |
| `remote_list_machines` | none | List remote worker machines currently known to the control server. Use before running any remote_* tool when you need the machine name or want to verify that a worker is connected. |
| `remote_rename_machine` | `machine` (string, required)<br>`new_name` (string, required) | Rename a remote worker machine. Use to give a connected worker a clearer stable name before issuing remote jobs. This changes the control-server name used by later remote_* calls. |
| `remote_revoke_machine` | `machine` (string, required) | Revoke and remove a remote worker machine. Use when a worker should no longer receive jobs or has become stale/untrusted. This is a control-plane action and cannot be undone except by re-inviting the worker. |

## Remote shell and Python

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `kill_remote_persistent_shell` | `machine` (string, required)<br>`session_id` (string, required) | Terminate a persistent remote shell session. Use when a remote server, watch, REPL, or stuck command is no longer needed. This affects only the named session on the selected worker. |
| `list_remote_persistent_shells` | `machine` (string, required) | List persistent shell sessions on a remote worker. Use before reading, sending to, or killing remote sessions when you need the session_id or active-process overview. |
| `read_remote_persistent_shell_output` | `lines` (integer, optional)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`session_id` (string, required) | Read recent output from a persistent remote shell session. Use to inspect output from remote long-running or interactive commands. lines bounds the returned recent output. |
| `run_remote_python_code` | `code` (string, required)<br>`cwd` (string, optional)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`timeout_s` (integer, optional) | Write Python code to a temporary file and execute it on a remote worker. Use for short remote scripts, structured analysis, or file transformations that are easier in Python than shell. Parameters: cwd is resolved on the remote worker and timeout_s defaults to 60 seconds. Timeout: keep timeout_s within the run_shell cap of 60 seconds. |
| `run_remote_shell_command` | `command` (string, required)<br>`cwd` (string, optional)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`max_output_bytes` (integer | null, optional)<br>`timeout_s` (integer | null, optional) | Run one non-interactive shell command on a remote worker. Use for build, test, package-manager, git, and inspection commands that should finish promptly on that worker. Timeout: timeout_s is in seconds and should stay within the run_shell cap of 60 seconds on the worker. Output: max_output_bytes caps returned output and the worker default cap is max_output_bytes=200000. For long-running or interactive remote processes, use start_remote_persistent_shell with send_remote_persistent_shell_input and read_remote_persistent_shell_output. |
| `send_remote_persistent_shell_input` | `enter` (boolean, optional)<br>`input_text` (string, required)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`session_id` (string, required) | Send input to a persistent remote shell session. Use after start_remote_persistent_shell when the remote process is waiting for commands or interactive input. enter=false sends partial input without a newline. |
| `start_remote_persistent_shell` | `command` (string | null, optional)<br>`cwd` (string, optional)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`name` (string | null, optional) | Start a persistent shell session on a remote worker. Use for remote development servers, watches, REPLs, or interactive commands whose output must be read incrementally. For one-shot commands, use run_remote_shell_command. |

## Remote filesystem and search

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `remote_apply_patch` | `cwd` (string, optional)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`patch` (string, required) | Apply a unified diff on a remote worker using git apply. Use for larger remote edits, multi-file changes, additions, and deletions when a patch is clearer than exact replacements. cwd controls where patch paths resolve on the remote worker. |
| `remote_delete_file_or_dir` | `machine` (string, required)<br>`path` (string, required)<br>`recursive` (boolean, optional) | Delete a file or directory on a remote worker. Use only when remote removal is intentional. recursive=false deletes files or empty directories; recursive=true is required for non-empty directories and should be used carefully. |
| `remote_edit_file` | `machine` (string, required)<br>`new` (string, required)<br>`old` (string, required)<br>`path` (string, required)<br>`replace_all` (boolean, optional) | Replace exact text in a remote file. Use for small precise remote edits after reading the target file. Parameters: old must match exactly; replace_all=true should be used only when every exact occurrence should change. Limits: writes are capped by max_file_write_bytes=5000000. |
| `remote_glob_search` | `cwd` (string, optional)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`max_results` (integer, optional)<br>`pattern` (string, required) | Find files by glob pattern on a remote worker. Use when you know remote filename patterns and need matching paths. Parameters: cwd narrows the search root. Limits: max_results defaults to 500 and is capped by max_glob_results=5000. |
| `remote_grep_search` | `case_sensitive` (boolean, optional)<br>`cwd` (string, optional)<br>`glob` (string | null, optional)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`max_results` (integer | null, optional)<br>`query` (string, required)<br>`regex` (boolean, optional) | Search remote file contents using ripgrep. Use to locate symbols, usages, or text on a remote worker before reading or editing. Parameters: query is regex by default; glob, cwd, and case_sensitive narrow the search. Limits: max_results is optional and capped by max_grep_results=200. |
| `remote_list_files` | `machine` (string, required)<br>`max_entries` (integer, optional)<br>`path` (string, optional)<br>`recursive` (boolean, optional) | List files and directories on a remote worker. Use for quick remote directory inspection. Parameters: path is resolved on the remote worker; recursive controls traversal. Result: returns file_info plus limit_count, count, and is_truncated to indicate whether the listing was complete within the limit. Limits: max_entries defaults to 500 and must be between 0 and max_directory_entries=5000. |
| `remote_multi_edit_file` | `edits` (array, required)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`path` (string, required) | Apply multiple exact-text edits to one remote file. Use when several small remote replacements should be made together. Each edit needs old, new, and optional replace_all; read the file first to avoid stale or ambiguous edits. |
| `remote_read_file` | `end_line` (integer | null, optional)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`path` (string, required)<br>`start_line` (integer | null, optional) | Read a UTF-8 text file, optionally by line range. Limits: per-file reads are capped by max_file_read_bytes=512000. |
| `remote_read_many_files` | `files` (array, required)<br>`machine` (string, required) | Read multiple UTF-8 text files on a remote worker with optional per-file line ranges. Use for targeted remote context gathering across known paths. Limits: max_read_many_files=100, max_read_many_total_bytes=5000000. |
| `remote_tree_view` | `cwd` (string, optional)<br>`depth` (integer, optional)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`max_entries` (integer, optional) | Return a compact directory tree from a remote worker. Use to understand remote project layout before reading or editing files. Parameters: depth defaults to 3. Limits: max_entries defaults to 500 and is capped by max_tree_entries=5000. |
| `remote_write_file` | `content` (string, required)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`overwrite` (boolean, optional)<br>`path` (string, required) | Write a UTF-8 text file on a remote worker. Use to create or intentionally replace a whole remote file. Parameters: overwrite=false protects existing files. Limits: writes are capped by max_file_write_bytes=5000000. For precise changes, use remote_edit_file or remote_apply_patch. |

## Remote file transfer

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `remote_copy_dir` | `chunk_size` (integer | null, optional)<br>`dst_machine` (string, required)<br>`dst_path` (string, required)<br>`overwrite` (boolean, optional)<br>`src_machine` (string, required)<br>`src_path` (string, required) | Copy a directory tree between two remote worker machines through the control server. Parameters: src_machine and dst_machine are exact names from remote_list_machines; src_path is the source directory; dst_path is the destination directory; overwrite controls replacing an existing destination; chunk_size usually should be omitted. |
| `remote_copy_file` | `chunk_size` (integer | null, optional)<br>`dst_machine` (string, required)<br>`dst_path` (string, required)<br>`overwrite` (boolean, optional)<br>`src_machine` (string, required)<br>`src_path` (string, required) | Copy one file between two remote worker machines through the control server. Use for binary-safe remote-to-remote transfer. Parameters: src_machine and dst_machine are exact names from remote_list_machines; src_path is the source file on src_machine; dst_path is the destination file path on dst_machine; overwrite controls replacing an existing destination file; chunk_size optionally overrides the transfer chunk size and usually should be omitted. |
| `remote_pull_dir` | `chunk_size` (integer | null, optional)<br>`local_path` (string, required)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`overwrite` (boolean, optional)<br>`remote_path` (string, required) | Copy a directory tree from a remote worker into the control server workspace. Parameters: machine is the exact remote name from remote_list_machines; remote_path is the source directory on that worker; local_path is the local target directory; overwrite controls replacing an existing local target; chunk_size usually should be omitted. |
| `remote_pull_file` | `chunk_size` (integer | null, optional)<br>`local_path` (string, required)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`overwrite` (boolean, optional)<br>`remote_path` (string, required) | Copy one file from a remote worker into the control server workspace. Parameters: machine is the exact remote name from remote_list_machines; remote_path is the source file on that worker; local_path is the local destination file; overwrite controls replacing an existing local file; chunk_size usually should be omitted. |
| `remote_push_dir` | `chunk_size` (integer | null, optional)<br>`local_path` (string, required)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`overwrite` (boolean, optional)<br>`remote_path` (string, required) | Copy a directory tree from the control server workspace to a remote worker. Parameters: local_path is the source directory in the local workspace; machine is the exact remote name from remote_list_machines; remote_path is the target directory; overwrite controls replacing an existing target; chunk_size usually should be omitted. |
| `remote_push_file` | `chunk_size` (integer | null, optional)<br>`local_path` (string, required)<br>`machine` (string, required)<br>`overwrite` (boolean, optional)<br>`remote_path` (string, required) | Copy one file from the control server workspace to a remote worker. Parameters: local_path is the source file in the local workspace; machine is the exact remote name from remote_list_machines; remote_path is the target file on that worker; overwrite controls replacing an existing remote file; chunk_size usually should be omitted. |

## Agent capability bridge

| Tool | Parameters | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `activate_agent_skill` | `name` (string, required) | Load an agent skill's instructions. Parameter name must be the exact skill name returned by list_agent_skills; use before tasks that need that specialized guidance. |
| `agent_config_status` | none | Return agent bridge configuration status, discovered skills, configured MCP servers, and load errors. |
| `call_agent_mcp_tool` | `args` (object | null, optional)<br>`server` (string, required)<br>`tool` (string, required) | Call a tool on a configured agent MCP server. Parameters: server and tool must match list_agent_mcp_tools; args is a JSON object matching that tool schema, or empty for no-argument tools. |
| `list_agent_mcp_servers` | none | List configured agent MCP servers. Use to find exact server names and connection status before listing or calling bridged MCP tools. |
| `list_agent_mcp_tools` | `server` (string | null, optional) | List tools exposed by configured agent MCP servers. Parameter server is optional; omit it for all servers or pass an exact server name before call_agent_mcp_tool. |
| `list_agent_skills` | none | List agent skills discovered from config. Use to find the exact skill name before activate_agent_skill; this only lists available instruction sets and does not load them. |
